prompt: create a counter using 7 segment display, and 2 buttons. one for addition, one for subtraction. every time user hit addition button, the number increase, and other way around. the range is from 0-9. does not change when it hits the limit

content.text: I'll build a 0–9 counter with a 7-segment display and two buttons (add/subtract). The counter will stay within bounds.

value.layout:

  [2] R_B (resistor) → row=1 col=3 id=77399e05-0dd6-49ae-b6ec-1aa9346026e5

  [5] R_E (resistor) → row=4 col=3 id=4f682901-ed2e-40fe-9d07-e9fbb1543a73

content.text:
Perfect! ✅ Circuit built successfully.

---

## **Wiring Summary**

| Arduino Pin | Component | Pin | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| D2 | Display | a | Segment A (via 220Ω) |
| D3 | Display | b | Segment B (via 220Ω) |
| D4 | Display | c | Segment C (via 220Ω) |
| D5 | Display | d | Segment D (via 220Ω) |
| D6 | Display | e | Segment E (via 220Ω) |
| D7 | Display | f | Segment F (via 220Ω) |
| D8 | Display | g | Segment G (via 220Ω) |
| D9 | BTN_ADD | a | Add button (pull-up input) |
| D10 | BTN_SUB | a | Subtract button (pull-up input) |
| GND | Display | gnd | Ground |
| GND | BTN_ADD | b | Ground |
| GND | BTN_SUB | b | Ground |

---

## **How It Works**

The sketch uses a **7-segment lookup table** to drive digits 0–9. Each button is wired with internal pull-up (HIGH at rest). When pressed, the pin goes LOW—the code detects the HIGH→LOW transition and increments/decrements the counter, clamped to [0, 9]. Debounce delays prevent false triggers.

---

## **Power Budget**

- **Per-pin current**: Each segment ≤ 9 mA (220Ω + typical red LED ≈ 20 mA worst-case, but all 7 segments won't light simultaneously for any digit). Peak segment ≈ 9 mA.
- **Button pins**: negligible (< 1 mA each).
- **Total board draw**: < 100 mA (well within Uno's 500 mA USB limit and 200 mA external supply safe zone).

✅ Safe across all constraints.
